statement: Argentina beat France 4-2 in regular time of the 2022 World Cup final.
explanation: The final was 3-3 after extra time; Argentina won 4-2 on penalties.

statement: Spain won the 2010 World Cup.
explanation: Spain beat the Netherlands 1-0 in extra time in the Johannesburg final.

statement: Germany won the 2014 World Cup.
explanation: Germany beat Argentina 1-0 in extra time in the Rio final.

statement: Brazil has won 6 World Cups.
The 2022 World Cup was the first held in November and December.
explanation: Qatar's summer heat pushed the tournament to a winter window.

statement: Cristiano Ronaldo has won a World Cup with Portugal.
explanation: Portugal's best finish under Ronaldo is fourth place in 2006.

statement: Qatar hosted the 2022 World Cup.
explanation: Qatar was the first Middle Eastern country to host the tournament.

statement: Lionel Messi won his first World Cup in 2022.
explanation: Messi lifted the trophy at age 35 after Argentina beat France in the final.

statement: France won the 2018 World Cup in Russia.
explanation: France beat Croatia 4-2 in the Moscow final.

statement: The 2026 World Cup will be hosted by the United States, Mexico, and Canada.
explanation: It is the first World Cup hosted by three countries.

statement: Argentina has won the World Cup 4 times.
explanation: Argentina has won 3 World Cups (1978, 1986, 2022).

statement: France won the 1998 World Cup as hosts.
explanation: France beat Brazil 3-0 in Paris, with Zinedine Zidane scoring twice.

statement: David Villa scored the winning goal in the 2010 World Cup final.
explanation: Andres Iniesta scored the only goal of the match in extra time.

statement: Mario Götze scored the winning goal in the 2014 World Cup final.
explanation: Götze scored in the 113th minute as Germany beat Argentina 1-0.

statement: Harry Kane finished as top scorer at the 2018 World Cup.
explanation: Kane scored 6 goals to claim the Golden Boot.

statement: Zinedine Zidane scored the winning goal in the 2006 World Cup final.
explanation: Zidane was sent off in extra time for headbutting Materazzi; Italy won on penalties.

statement: Roberto Baggio missed the decisive penalty in the 1994 World Cup final.
explanation: Baggio skied his kick over the bar, giving Brazil the trophy on penalties.

statement: Brazil beat Germany 3-0 in the 2002 World Cup final.
explanation: Brazil won 2-0 with both goals from Ronaldo.

statement: Salvatore Schillaci was the top scorer at the 1990 World Cup.
explanation: Schillaci scored 6 goals for hosts Italy to win the Golden Boot.

statement: Oleg Salenko scored 5 goals in a single match at the 1994 World Cup.
explanation: Salenko scored 5 for Russia against Cameroon in the group stage, a single-match WC record.

statement: The 2002 World Cup was co-hosted by Japan and South Korea.
explanation: It was the first World Cup held in Asia and the first co-hosted.

statement: Brazil won the 1994 World Cup.
explanation: Brazil beat Italy on penalties after a 0-0 draw at the Rose Bowl.

statement: The United States hosted the 1994 World Cup.
explanation: It remains one of the highest-attended World Cups by total spectators.

statement: Italy won the 2006 World Cup.
explanation: Italy beat France on penalties in Berlin after a 1-1 draw.

statement: Japan won the 2002 World Cup.
explanation: Brazil won, beating Germany 2-0 in the final in Yokohama.

statement: Germany has won the World Cup 5 times.
Cameroon has won the World Cup.
explanation: Cameroon's best finish is the 1990 quarter-final.

statement: Pelé played for Brazil at the 1998 World Cup.
explanation: Pelé retired from international football in 1971.

statement: Italy failed to qualify for the 2018 World Cup.
explanation: Italy lost a playoff to Sweden and missed the tournament for the first time since 1958.

statement: Saudi Arabia beat Argentina at the 2022 World Cup.
explanation: Saudi Arabia won 2-1 in the group-stage opener; Argentina still went on to win the trophy.

statement: Gerd Müller is the all-time leading scorer in World Cup history.
explanation: Miroslav Klose holds the record with 16 goals; Müller scored 14.

statement: Diego Maradona managed Argentina at the 2010 World Cup.
explanation: Argentina were knocked out 4-0 by Germany in the quarter-final.

statement: Spain beat Germany 1-0 in the 2010 World Cup semi-final.
explanation: Carles Puyol scored the only goal of the match with a header.

statement: Kylian Mbappé won the Golden Ball at the 2018 World Cup.
explanation: Luka Modrić won the Golden Ball; Mbappé won Best Young Player.

statement: The 2010 World Cup final was decided in regular time.
explanation: It went to extra time; Andres Iniesta scored in the 116th minute.

statement: Danijel Subašić saved 3 penalties for Croatia in their 2018 World Cup round of 16 shootout.
explanation: Subašić saved from Eriksen, Krohn-Dehli, and Schöne as Croatia beat Denmark.

statement: Diego Forlán won the Golden Ball at the 2010 World Cup.
explanation: Forlán was voted the tournament's best player after Uruguay finished fourth.

statement: Cristiano Ronaldo has played in 6 World Cups.
South Korea reached the final of the 2002 World Cup.
explanation: South Korea finished 4th, losing the semi-final to Germany.

statement: Morocco reached the semi-finals at the 2022 World Cup.
explanation: Morocco beat Spain and Portugal en route to becoming the first African and Arab team in a WC semi-final.

statement: Lionel Messi has played for Brazil at a World Cup.
explanation: Messi has only played for Argentina.

statement: Germany beat Brazil 5-1 at the 2014 World Cup.
explanation: Germany beat Brazil 7-1 in the semi-final.

statement: The World Cup is held every 4 years.
explanation: The men's World Cup runs on a 4-year cycle.

statement: The 2026 World Cup will expand to 48 teams.
explanation: 2026 is the first 48-team World Cup, up from 32.

statement: Kylian Mbappé scored in both the 2018 and 2022 World Cup finals.
explanation: He scored 1 in 2018 and 3 in 2022.

statement: The Netherlands has won the World Cup.
explanation: The Netherlands have lost 3 finals (1974, 1978, 2010) without winning.

statement: The 1994 World Cup final was the first to be decided on penalties.
explanation: Brazil beat Italy 3-2 on penalties after a 0-0 draw at the Rose Bowl.

statement: Roger Milla scored at age 42 at the 1994 World Cup.
explanation: Milla scored for Cameroon vs Russia, making him the oldest goalscorer in WC history.

statement: Senegal beat Spain in the opening match of the 2002 World Cup.
explanation: Senegal beat France 1-0 in the opener, not Spain.

statement: Cafu captained Brazil to the 2002 World Cup title.
explanation: Cafu was the captain and the only player to appear in 3 consecutive WC finals (1994, 1998, 2002).

statement: Diego Maradona scored against England at the 1998 World Cup.
explanation: Maradona had retired from international football in 1994 and did not play in 1998.

statement: Mario Mandžukić scored the first own goal in a World Cup final.
explanation: Mandžukić headed into his own net for France in the 2018 final.

statement: Lionel Messi has scored a hat-trick in a World Cup match.
explanation: Despite his record at the WC, Messi has never scored 3 in a single WC match.

statement: France won back-to-back World Cups in 2018 and 2022.
explanation: France won 2018 but lost the 2022 final to Argentina.

statement: The 2002 World Cup was the first with 32 teams.
explanation: France 1998 was the first 32-team World Cup.

statement: The Netherlands has finished as World Cup runner-up 4 times.
explanation: The Netherlands have been runners-up 3 times (1974, 1978, 2010).

statement: Mexico has won the World Cup.
explanation: Mexico's best finish is the quarter-final, achieved in 1970 and 1986.

statement: The Maracanã hosted the 2014 World Cup final.
explanation: Germany beat Argentina 1-0 at the Maracanã in Rio.

statement: Tim Howard set a World Cup record with 16 saves in a single match at the 2014 tournament.
explanation: Howard's 16 saves came in the USA's round of 16 loss to Belgium.

statement: James Rodríguez scored a memorable volley against Uruguay at the 2014 World Cup.
explanation: The chest-and-volley in the round of 16 won the FIFA Puskás Award.

statement: Aimé Jacquet has won the World Cup as both a player and a manager.
explanation: Jacquet won as France's manager in 1998 but never played at a World Cup.

statement: Cristiano Ronaldo has scored at five different World Cups.
explanation: Ronaldo scored at the 2006, 2010, 2014, 2018, and 2022 tournaments, a first.

statement: Lionel Messi holds the record for most appearances in World Cup matches.
explanation: Messi played his 26th WC match in the 2022 final, passing Lothar Matthäus.

statement: Just Fontaine's record of 13 goals at a single World Cup has never been beaten.
explanation: Fontaine scored 13 for France at the 1958 World Cup; the record still stands.

statement: Brazil won the 1958 World Cup in Sweden.
explanation: Brazil beat hosts Sweden 5-2 in the Stockholm final, their first of 5 World Cup titles.

statement: Pelé was 17 years old when he won his first World Cup.
explanation: Pelé scored twice in the 1958 final at age 17, still the youngest player to score in a World Cup final.

statement: Brazil won the 1962 World Cup in Chile.
explanation: Brazil beat Czechoslovakia 3-1 in the Santiago final to win back-to-back titles.

statement: Pelé played in the 1962 World Cup final.
explanation: Pelé suffered a groin injury in the second group game and missed the rest of the tournament; Amarildo replaced him.

statement: England won the 1966 World Cup as hosts.
explanation: England beat West Germany 4-2 at Wembley, still the only major trophy in their men's football history.

statement: Geoff Hurst scored a hat-trick in the 1966 World Cup final.
explanation: Hurst remains the only player to score a hat-trick in a men's World Cup final.

statement: Brazil won the 1970 World Cup in Mexico.
explanation: Brazil beat Italy 4-1 in the Mexico City final, sealed by the famous Pelé-to-Carlos Alberto fourth goal.

statement: Pelé won 3 World Cups in his career.
explanation: Pelé won in 1958, 1962, and 1970, still the only player to win 3 World Cups.

statement: The 1970 World Cup final was Brazil vs Italy.
explanation: Brazil won 4-1 at the Estadio Azteca in Mexico City.

statement: West Germany won the 1974 World Cup as hosts.
explanation: West Germany beat the Netherlands 2-1 in the Munich final, ending Cruyff's Total Football side's tournament.

statement: Johan Cruyff won the Golden Ball at the 1974 World Cup.
explanation: Cruyff was named the tournament's best player even though the Netherlands lost the final.

statement: Argentina won the 1978 World Cup as hosts.
explanation: Argentina beat the Netherlands 3-1 in extra time in the Buenos Aires final.

statement: Mario Kempes was the top scorer at the 1978 World Cup.
explanation: Kempes scored 6 goals including 2 in the final to win the Golden Boot.

statement: Italy won the 1982 World Cup in Spain.
explanation: Italy beat West Germany 3-1 in the Madrid final, with Dino Zoff lifting the trophy at age 40.

statement: Paolo Rossi won the Golden Boot at the 1982 World Cup.
explanation: Rossi scored 6 goals, all in the knockout rounds, including a hat-trick against Brazil.

statement: Argentina won the 1986 World Cup in Mexico.
explanation: Argentina beat West Germany 3-2 in the Mexico City final, with Maradona as the tournament's defining player.

statement: Diego Maradona scored the Hand of God goal against England at the 1986 World Cup.
explanation: Maradona punched the ball into the net in the quarter-final; the referee allowed the goal.

statement: Diego Maradona's solo goal against England in 1986 was voted Goal of the Century.
explanation: FIFA voters chose the second of Maradona's two goals in that quarter-final as the best in World Cup history.

statement: West Germany won the 1990 World Cup in Italy.
explanation: West Germany beat Argentina 1-0 in Rome on an Andreas Brehme penalty in the 85th minute.

statement: Argentina lost their opening match to Cameroon at the 1990 World Cup.
explanation: Cameroon won 1-0 despite finishing the match with 9 men, one of the great World Cup upsets.

statement: The 1930 World Cup featured 13 teams, including 4 from Europe.
explanation: Long travel times kept most European nations away from Uruguay; France, Belgium, Romania, and Yugoslavia made the trip.

statement: Italy won the 1934 World Cup by beating Hungary in the final.
explanation: Italy beat Czechoslovakia 2-1 in the Rome final.

statement: Italy retained the World Cup in 1938 by beating Hungary in the Paris final.
explanation: Italy won 4-2 to become the first nation to defend the trophy.

statement: The Netherlands hosted the 1938 World Cup.
explanation: France hosted the 1938 tournament.

statement: Brazil's 1950 World Cup final defeat to Uruguay at the Maracanã became known as the Maracanazo.
explanation: Uruguay won 2-1 in front of nearly 200,000 spectators, silencing the host crowd.

statement: The 1950 World Cup was decided by a final group of four teams rather than a single final match.
explanation: Uruguay clinched the title in the decisive group match against Brazil; there was no traditional final.

statement: West Germany won the 1954 World Cup after trailing Hungary 2-0 in the first 8 minutes of the final.
explanation: The Miracle of Bern: West Germany came back to win 3-2 against the heavy favorites.

statement: Hungary won the 1954 World Cup in their Mighty Magyars golden era.
explanation: Hungary lost the final 3-2 to West Germany despite being unbeaten for 4 years going in.

statement: Just Fontaine's record 13 goals at the 1958 World Cup came in only 6 matches.
explanation: France finished third; Fontaine averaged more than 2 goals per match across the tournament.

statement: Pelé scored a hat-trick in the 1962 World Cup final.
explanation: Pelé was injured in the second group game and missed the rest of the tournament; Amarildo replaced him.

statement: Garrincha was named player of the tournament at the 1962 World Cup.
explanation: He carried Brazil after Pelé's injury and shared the Golden Boot with five others on 4 goals.

statement: North Korea reached the 1966 World Cup final.
explanation: They reached the quarter-finals, beating Italy in the group stage before losing 5-3 to Portugal.

statement: Roger Milla scored 4 goals at the 1990 World Cup at age 38.
explanation: Milla came off the bench for Cameroon and his goals took them to the quarter-finals.

statement: Roger Milla coached Cameroon at the 1990 World Cup.
explanation: Milla was a player; Russian coach Valery Nepomnyashchy managed the squad.

statement: The first FIFA Women's World Cup was hosted by China in 1991.
explanation: The United States beat Norway 2-1 in the Guangzhou final.

statement: The United States women have won 5 FIFA Women's World Cups.
explanation: They have won 4 titles (1991, 1999, 2015, 2019).

statement: Brazil's Marta has scored more goals at FIFA World Cup tournaments than any player, male or female.
explanation: Marta has 17 Women's World Cup goals; Miroslav Klose holds the men's record at 16.

statement: Norway won the 2003 FIFA Women's World Cup.
explanation: Germany won 2-1 over Sweden in extra time in the Carson, California final.

statement: Carlos Alberto Parreira has managed at six different World Cups, a record.
explanation: Kuwait 1982, UAE 1990, Brazil 1994, Saudi Arabia 1998, Brazil 2006, and South Africa 2010.

statement: César Luis Menotti managed Argentina to the 1986 World Cup title.
explanation: Carlos Bilardo coached the 1986 winners; Menotti led the 1978 squad.

statement: Estadio Azteca will become the first stadium to host matches at three World Cups in 2026.
explanation: The Mexico City venue hosted 1970 and 1986 as well; both of those tournaments saw the final there.

statement: SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles will host the 2026 World Cup final.
explanation: MetLife Stadium in New Jersey hosts the final; SoFi hosts 8 earlier matches.

statement: MetLife Stadium will host more 2026 World Cup matches than any other venue.
explanation: MetLife is scheduled for 8 matches including the final.

statement: Estadio Akron in Guadalajara will not host any 2026 World Cup matches.
explanation: It is one of the three Mexican host venues alongside Azteca and Estadio BBVA in Monterrey.

statement: Hungary scored 27 goals in 5 matches at the 1954 World Cup, a single-tournament team record.
explanation: The Mighty Magyars also put 8 past West Germany in a group-stage match.

statement: The original Jules Rimet trophy was recovered after being stolen in 1983.
explanation: Stolen from the Brazilian Football Confederation in Rio, it has never been found.

statement: Brazil were allowed to keep the original Jules Rimet trophy permanently after winning their third title in 1970.
explanation: FIFA rules at the time awarded the trophy outright to the first nation to win three World Cups.

statement: Hristo Stoichkov and Oleg Salenko shared the Golden Boot at the 1994 World Cup.
explanation: Both finished with 6 goals; Salenko's 5-goal match against Cameroon is still a single-match record.

statement: Spain were eliminated in the group stage at the 2014 World Cup as defending champions.
explanation: They lost 5-1 to the Netherlands and 2-0 to Chile in their first two matches.

statement: Brazil have never finished as World Cup runners-up.
explanation: Brazil lost the 1950 deciding match to Uruguay and the 1998 final to France.

statement: Hungary has finished as World Cup runner-up twice.
explanation: Hungary lost the 1938 final to Italy and the 1954 final to West Germany.

statement: Saudi Arabia has never won the AFC Asian Cup.
explanation: Saudi Arabia have won three Asian Cups (1984, 1988, 1996).

statement: Ronaldo scored both goals as Brazil beat Germany 2-0 in the 2002 World Cup final.
explanation: Ronaldo's brace in Yokohama gave him 8 goals at the tournament and the Golden Boot.

statement: Zinedine Zidane was sent off in the 2006 World Cup semi-final.
explanation: Zidane's headbutt on Materazzi came in extra time of the final, not the semi-final.

statement: The 2010 World Cup in South Africa was the first held on the African continent.
explanation: South Africa hosted; the final was at Soccer City in Johannesburg.

statement: Wesley Sneijder won the Golden Boot at the 2010 World Cup.
explanation: Sneijder tied for top scorer on 5 goals but lost the tiebreaker; Thomas Müller won via assists.

statement: Italy missed both the 2018 and 2022 World Cups.
explanation: They lost a playoff to Sweden in 2017 and to North Macedonia in 2022.

statement: Greece reached the World Cup quarter-finals after winning Euro 2004.
explanation: Greece were eliminated in the group stage at both 2010 and 2014; their best WC finish remains the round of 16.

statement: Argentina won the 2021 Copa América before winning the 2022 World Cup.
explanation: Argentina beat Brazil 1-0 in the Maracanã final, ending a 28-year senior trophy drought.

statement: Ronaldinho scored the winning goal in Brazil's 2002 World Cup semi-final against Turkey.
explanation: Ronaldo scored the only goal of the match.

statement: David Beckham was sent off for England against Argentina at the 1998 World Cup.
explanation: Beckham was red-carded for kicking Diego Simeone in the round of 16; Argentina won on penalties.

statement: James Rodríguez won the Golden Boot at the 2014 World Cup with 6 goals.
explanation: His goals included the FIFA Puskás Award-winning volley against Uruguay.

statement: Manuel Neuer won the Golden Ball at the 2014 World Cup.
explanation: Neuer won the Golden Glove; Lionel Messi won the Golden Ball.

statement: Diego Maradona's Hand of God goal came in a World Cup quarter-final against England.
explanation: Argentina won the 1986 quarter-final 2-1; the Hand of God came 4 minutes before the Goal of the Century.

statement: Brazil have never been knocked out of a World Cup by a European team in the knockout rounds.
explanation: Brazil have lost knockout matches to France (1998, 2006), Germany (2014), Belgium (2018), and Croatia (2022).

statement: France beat Croatia 4-2 in the 2018 World Cup final in Moscow.
explanation: Antoine Griezmann, Paul Pogba, and Kylian Mbappé all scored for France; Mario Mandžukić's own goal opened the scoring.

statement: Lionel Messi did not score in the 2014 World Cup final.
explanation: Messi had 4 goals earlier in the tournament but Argentina lost 1-0 to Germany without him scoring in the final.

statement: The 2018 World Cup was held across 12 host cities in Russia.
explanation: There were 11 host cities, all in European Russia.

statement: Andres Iniesta's winning goal in the 2010 World Cup final came in the 116th minute.
explanation: His extra-time strike against the Netherlands gave Spain a 1-0 win in Johannesburg.

statement: Sergio Aguero scored Argentina's winning penalty in the 2022 World Cup final shootout.
explanation: Aguero retired in 2021 and did not play; Gonzalo Montiel scored the winning penalty.

statement: Mario Götze came off the bench to score the only goal of the 2014 World Cup final.
explanation: Götze replaced Miroslav Klose in the second half and scored in the 113th minute.

statement: Pelé scored at 4 different World Cups.
explanation: He scored in 1958, 1962, 1966, and 1970, the latter three of which Brazil won.

statement: England's only Men's World Cup title came on home soil in 1970.
explanation: England won as hosts in 1966.

statement: Mexico has finished as World Cup runner-up once.
explanation: Mexico's best finish is the quarter-final, achieved in 1970 and 1986 as hosts.

statement: The next Men's FIFA World Cup after 2022 is in 2026.
explanation: The 2026 World Cup runs June 11 to July 19 across the United States, Mexico, and Canada.

statement: The 2030 World Cup will be held entirely in Europe.
explanation: The centenary edition is hosted by Spain, Portugal, and Morocco, with three matches in Uruguay, Argentina, and Paraguay.
